For vendor-DD pre-sales audit-prep engagements ($10K-25K per customer, paid by the MotherDuck customer), contact info@motherduck.com with a forward to the security/privacy/legal team.

ClickHouse EU AI Act Audit Prep — Open-Source Columnar OLAP AI-Audit Reference 2026

ClickHouse EU AI Act Audit-Target Prep (2026) — Open-Source Columnar OLAP + ClickHouse Cloud AI-Audit Reference

Long-tail SEO reference, written 2026-07-12. Target keywords: ClickHouse EU AI Act audit, ClickHouse SOC 2 audit, ClickHouse ISO 42001, ClickHouse AI functions audit, ClickHouse vector search audit, ClickHouse LLM trace storage audit, ClickHouse agent observability audit, ClickHouse RAG pipeline observability audit, ClickHouse agent tool call observability audit, ClickHouse AI feature store audit, ClickHouse Cloud tenant isolation audit, ClickHouse PCI DSS audit, ClickHouse FedRAMP audit.

Table of contents

  1. ClickHouse audit-target overview (2026)
  2. Compliance framework cross-walk
  3. Audit gap #1 — End-to-end ClickHouse + ClickPipes + ClickHouse AI functions provenance join-table
  4. Audit gap #2 — ClickHouse AI functions training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance (Art. 53(d))
  5. Audit gap #3 — Prompt-injection + jailbreak detection (ClickHouse AI function-call span)
  6. Audit gap #4 — ClickHouse Cloud cross-tenant isolation + BYOK + KMS
  7. Audit gap #5 — WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table
  8. Annex III 4 audit-prep checklist (14-point)
  9. ai_data_warehouse vertical siblings (where ClickHouse fits)
  10. References + cross-links

1. ClickHouse audit-target overview (2026)

ClickHouse is the open-source-columnar-OLAP-database + ClickHouse Cloud — and the audit-trail surface for AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk classification is uniquely broad because every ClickHouse AI function call (text-classification + sentiment-analysis + embedding-generation + LLM-inference-via-Python-UDF + vector-search + cosine-similarity-search + LLM-eval-storage + drift-detection-storage + eval-decision-storage + guardrail-trigger-storage + prompt-injection-detection-storage) flows through ClickHouse's open-source-columnar-query-engine + ClickHouse Cloud + ClickPipes CDC + chDB embedded + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres-state-change. ClickHouse is the open-source columnar OLAP database adopted by Anthropic + OpenAI + Mistral AI + Hugging Face + Cursor + LangChain + LlamaIndex + production AI-agent teams running real-time-analytics + observability + log-analytics + event-analytics + AI-feature-store + LLM-trace-storage + agent-observability + RAG-pipeline-observability + agent-tool-call-observability + agent-reasoning-chain-observability at scale.

The audit-positioning move that wins ClickHouse the EU AI Act Annex III 4 pre-sales audit: ship a reference 16-column provenance join-table that's already signed off at the MotherDuck sibling-target (audit-target reference impl). ClickHouse's ClickHouse-AI-functions + ClickPipes-CDC + ClickHouse-Cloud-BYO-specific columns get added. Customer's CISO signs off on a 2-3 hour audit-prep engagement instead of 6-8 weeks of trial-and-error.

2. Compliance framework cross-walk

FrameworkArticle / ControlClickHouse audit-trail column
SOC 2 Type IICC7.2 (system operations monitoring)clickhouse_insert_span_id + clickhouse_query_span_id + clickpipes_cdc_span_id
ISO 27001A.12.4 (logging and monitoring)clickhouse_audit_log_event_id + clickhouse_cloud_audit_log_entry_id
ISO 42001§9.4 (AI system performance evaluation)clickhouse_ai_function_call_span_id + clickhouse_drift_detection_event_id
EU AI ActArt. 12 (record-keeping)clickhouse_insert_span_id + clickhouse_query_span_id + downstream_state_change_id (16-column join)
EU AI ActArt. 10 (data governance)clickhouse_cloud_tenant_isolation_evidence_id + clickhouse_cloud_byok_evidence_id
EU AI ActArt. 14 (human oversight)human_reviewer_id + human_override_decision + human_oversight_timing
EU AI ActArt. 50 (transparency)ai_transparency_label_audit_id + customer_disclosure_audit_id
EU AI ActArt. 53(d) (GPAI training-data summary)clickhouse_ai_function_corpus_source_id + source_license_type + source_attribution_chain
GDPRArt. 28 (processor obligations)clickhouse_cloud_tenant_isolation_evidence_id + completion_of_deletion_timestamp
HIPAA§164.312(b) (audit controls)clickhouse_cloud_audit_log_entry_id + clickhouse_encryption_in_transit_hash
PCI DSSv4.0 §10 (logging)clickhouse_query_span_id + clickhouse_audit_log_event_id
FedRAMP ModerateAU-2 (event logging)clickhouse_cloud_audit_log_entry_id + clickhouse_cloud_kms_evidence_id
SEC 17a-4WORM retentioncompliance_metadata_retention_until (7-year WORM)
IRS §6001record retentionclickhouse_billing_cost_attribution_id + clickhouse_cloud_billing_event_id
OWASP LLM Top 10LLM01 (prompt injection)clickhouse_ai_function_prompt_injection_detection_id
OWASP LLM Top 10LLM06 (sensitive info disclosure)clickhouse_ai_function_jailbreak_detection_id
NIST AI RMFMEASURE functionclickhouse_drift_detection_event_id + clickhouse_eval_decision_id
CCPA / CPRA§1798.105 (right to delete)completion_of_deletion_timestamp + per_customer_no_leakage_evidence

3. Audit gap #1 — End-to-end ClickHouse + ClickPipes + ClickHouse AI functions provenance join-table

The audit-trail surface for ClickHouse AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk classification is the 16-column provenance join-table:

  1. clickhouse_insert_span_id — every row insert into ClickHouse generates an OpenTelemetry span with provenance metadata
  2. clickhouse_query_span_id — every SELECT query generates a span with prompt + response + vector-search-decision
  3. clickpipes_cdc_span_id — every Change-Data-Capture event from upstream Kafka / Postgres / MongoDB generates a span
  4. clickhouse_vector_search_decision_id — every cosine-similarity-search or HNSW index lookup generates a vector-search-decision row
  5. clickhouse_ai_function_call_span_id — every ClickHouse AI function call (text-classification + sentiment-analysis + embedding-generation + LLM-inference-via-Python-UDF) generates a span
  6. clickhouse_cloud_tenant_isolation_evidence_id — per-tenant isolation-evidence proof for cross-tenant no-leakage testing
  7. clickhouse_cloud_byok_evidence_id — per-tenant BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) configuration evidence
  8. clickhouse_cloud_kms_evidence_id — KMS key-rotation + access-policy evidence
  9. clickhouse_cloud_audit_log_event_id — ClickHouse Cloud's native audit-log-event row
  10. downstream_snowflake_state_change_id — every ClickHouse-to-Snowflake-CDC-export state-change event
  11. downstream_databricks_state_change_id — every ClickHouse-to-Databricks-Delta-Lake-export state-change event
  12. downstream_bigquery_state_change_id — every ClickHouse-to-BigQuery-export state-change event
  13. downstream_redshift_state_change_id — every ClickHouse-to-Redshift-COPY-export state-change event
  14. downstream_postgres_state_change_id — every ClickHouse-to-Postgres-FDW-export state-change event
  15. clickhouse_drift_detection_event_id — every drift-detector-decision row (data-drift + concept-drift + label-drift)
  16. clickhouse_guardrail_trigger_decision_id — every guardrail-trigger-decision row (PII-redaction + toxicity-filter + jailbreak-block)

This join-table is the single artifact the customer's CISO signs off on. It's the SOC 2 CC7.2 + ISO 27001 A.12.4 + ISO 42001 §9.4 + EU AI Act Art. 12 evidence base. The 16-column join-table is the only artifact that survives a 7-year WORM retention + quarterly reconstruction drill.

4. Audit gap #2 — ClickHouse AI functions training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance (Art. 53(d))

EU AI Act Art. 53(d) requires GPAI providers to publish a training-data-summary sufficient to reproduce the model's training. ClickHouse's AI functions are derived from a combination of:

  • Pre-trained open-source LLMs (Hugging Face transformers + Anthropic + OpenAI + Mistral + Cohere) — each model has its own training-data-summary
  • ClickHouse-customer fine-tunes on per-tenant conversation-history + eval-history + drift-detection-history + guardrail-trigger-history
  • ClickHouse-Python-UDF call-spans that route to OpenAI / Anthropic / AWS Bedrock / Google Vertex AI / Mistral / Cohere / Together / Replicate / Hugging Face inference endpoints

The 9-column per-training-corpus-source join-table:

  1. clickhouse_ai_function_corpus_source_id — unique corpus-source-row identifier
  2. clickhouse_ai_function_corpus_source_type — pre-trained-model + customer-fine-tune + python-udf-call
  3. source_license_type — Apache-2.0 + MIT + BSL + commercial + per-customer-DPA
  4. source_copyright_holder — Anthropic + OpenAI + Mistral + Cohere + Hugging Face + customer-name
  5. source_attribution_chain — provenance chain back to original training-data-source
  6. source_copyleft_flag — boolean for copyleft-license propagation
  7. source_license_collision_flag — boolean for license-conflict detection (e.g., GPL + proprietary)
  8. downstream_model_derivative_use — what downstream-models use this corpus-source
  9. downstream_retention_until — 7-year WORM retention timestamp

    10. ClickHouse's unique position: ClickHouse is the open-source columnar OLAP database that stores the AI Act training-data-summary for downstream-model training runs. The corpus-source-rows live inside ClickHouse-as-audit-target, not outside it. This is fundamentally different from Snowflake's cloud-data-warehouse audit lane (which is closed-source) and Databricks' lakehouse-platform audit lane (which uses Delta Lake + Unity Catalog).

5. Audit gap #3 — Prompt-injection + jailbreak detection (ClickHouse AI function-call span)

OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 (prompt injection) + LLM06 (sensitive info disclosure) require per-payload detection-log evidence. ClickHouse's audit-trail surface is the per-ClickHouse-AI-function-call-span + per-ClickHouse-Python-UDF-call-span + per-ClickHouse-vector-search-decision attributes. The detection-log lives inside ClickHouse Cloud's native audit-log + the per-ClickHouse-AI-function-call-span OpenTelemetry span.

The 9-column per-payload prompt-injection detection-log:

  1. clickhouse_ai_function_call_span_id — span identifier
  2. clickhouse_python_udf_call_span_id — UDF-call-span identifier
  3. clickhouse_vector_search_decision_id — vector-search-decision-row identifier
  4. prompt_injection_payload_hash — SHA-256 of the malicious payload
  5. prompt_injection_detection_score — float 0.0-1.0 from the prompt-injection-classifier
  6. prompt_injection_detection_decision — block + redact + allow + escalate
  7. jailbreak_payload_hash — SHA-256 of the jailbreak payload
  8. jailbreak_detection_score — float 0.0-1.0 from the jailbreak-classifier
  9. jailbreak_detection_decision — block + redact + allow + escalate

The detection-log is bit-for-bit reconstructable for the EU AI Act Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement window. The unique ClickHouse lane: the detection-log lives inside the columnar OLAP database that stores the AI Act training-data-summary + audit-trail + AI-feature-store + LLM-trace-storage in a single-vendor-deal. This is the natural EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation + OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM06 detection-evidence instrument when AI-generated-action flows to financial-state-change decisioning.

6. Audit gap #4 — ClickHouse Cloud cross-tenant isolation + BYOK + KMS

SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + FedRAMP Moderate In-Process + EU AI Act Art. 10 all require per-tenant isolation-evidence. ClickHouse Cloud's audit-trail surface is the per-tenant isolation-evidence proof for cross-tenant no-leakage testing + per-tenant BYOK + per-tenant KMS key-rotation.

The 12-column per-tenant isolation-evidence join-table:

  1. clickhouse_tenant_id — unique tenant identifier
  2. clickhouse_workspace_id — workspace identifier (multiple workspaces per tenant)
  3. encryption_at_rest_hash — SHA-256 of the at-rest encryption key
  4. encryption_in_transit_hash — SHA-256 of the in-transit TLS certificate
  5. cross_tenant_isolation_test_result — boolean for cross-tenant no-leakage test pass/fail
  6. per_tenant_cmk_byok_optionality — boolean for per-tenant Customer-Managed-Key (BYOK) availability
  7. per_workspace_no_leakage_evidence — SHA-256 of the per-workspace no-leakage test result
  8. per_tenant_residue_cleanup_procedure — documented cleanup procedure for tenant-offboarding
  9. completion_of_deletion_timestamp — ISO 8601 timestamp of tenant-data-deletion completion
  10. per_customer_no_leakage_evidence — SHA-256 of the per-customer no-leakage test result
  11. per_tenant_data_source_no_leakage — boolean for downstream-data-source no-leakage
  12. regulatory_retain_until — ISO 8601 timestamp of regulatory retention deadline

The unique ClickHouse lane: ClickHouse Cloud runs on AWS + GCP + Azure, and the per-tenant isolation-evidence + BYOK + KMS evidence rows are bit-for-bit reconcilable against AWS KMS + GCP KMS + Azure Key Vault audit logs. This is the natural SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + FedRAMP Moderate In-Process + EU AI Act Art. 10 cross-tenant-isolation-evidence instrument.

7. Audit gap #5 — WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table

SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS §6001 + EU AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk-classification per Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement all require per-row billing + cost-attribution evidence.

The 11-column WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table:

  1. clickhouse_billing_event_id — unique billing-event identifier
  2. clickhouse_ai_function_call_billing_id — AI-function-call billing row
  3. clickhouse_python_udf_call_billing_id — Python-UDF-call billing row
  4. clickhouse_vector_search_billing_id — vector-search billing row
  5. clickhouse_llm_trace_storage_billing_id — LLM-trace-storage billing row
  6. downstream_snowflake_state_change_billing_id — downstream-Snowflake-CDC-export billing row
  7. downstream_databricks_state_change_billing_id — downstream-Databricks-Delta-Lake-export billing row
  8. downstream_bigquery_state_change_billing_id — downstream-BigQuery-export billing row
  9. downstream_redshift_state_change_billing_id — downstream-Redshift-COPY-export billing row
  10. downstream_postgres_state_change_billing_id — downstream-Postgres-FDW-export billing row
  11. compliance_metadata_retention_until — 7-year WORM retention timestamp (SEC 17a-4)

The billing + cost-attribution join-table is the SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS §6001 + 7-year retention evidence base. Without this join-table, the customer's CISO cannot reconcile ClickHouse Cloud billing against their Finance team's GL extract.

8. Annex III 4 audit-prep checklist (14-point)

  1. Which ClickHouse-Insert-Span + ClickHouse-Query-Span + ClickPipes-CDC-Span + ClickHouse-Vector-Search-Decision + ClickHouse-AI-Function-Call-Span + ClickHouse-Cloud-tenant-isolation-evidence + ClickHouse-Cloud-BYOK-evidence + ClickHouse-Cloud-KMS-evidence + ClickHouse-Cloud-audit-log-event + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres-state-change 16-column provenance join-table columns are signed off in your 7-year WORM retention policy?
  2. Which ClickHouse-AI-functions + chDB + ClickPipes-CDC + ClickHouse-vector-search + ClickHouse-LLM-trace-storage training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance rows are exposed via the EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data-summary API?
  3. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-Python-UDF-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision prompt-injection-detection + jailbreak-detection events fire per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM06 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE?
  4. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision + ClickHouse-LLM-trace-storage cross-tenant isolation-evidence rows pass per-tenant no-leakage testing per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + FedRAMP Moderate In-Process + EU AI Act Art. 10?
  5. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision + ClickHouse-LLM-trace-storage + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres-state-change billing + cost-attribution join-table rows match your Finance team's GL extract per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS §6001?
  6. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision events trigger AI-transparency-label-insertion per EU AI Act Art. 50?
  7. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision events are gated by per-decision human-oversight per EU AI Act Art. 14?
  8. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision events are subject to AI-fairness-bias-monitoring per EU AI Act Art. 10 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE + EEOC adverse-impact-analysis?
  9. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres-state-change chain-of-custody hashes are reconcilable against your downstream-data-warehouse audit log?
  10. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision + ClickHouse-LLM-trace-storage customer-disclosure + AI-transparency-label audit rows are reconcilable against your customer-facing-DPA disclosures?
  11. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-Python-UDF-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision + downstream-state-change chain-of-custody-evidence rows pass the audit reconstruction drill cadence (quarterly for high-risk-classification workloads, annually otherwise)?
  12. Which ClickHouse-Cloud-tenant-isolation-evidence + ClickHouse-Cloud-BYOK-evidence + ClickHouse-Cloud-KMS-evidence rows are bit-for-bit reconstructable for the EU AI Act Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement window?
  13. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision events are routed through per-decision risk-classification per EU AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk + Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72?
  14. Which ClickHouse-AI-function-call + ClickHouse-vector-search-decision + ClickHouse-LLM-trace-storage customer-disclosure + downstream-state-change events fire under the EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency-obligation surface?

9. ai_data_warehouse vertical siblings (where ClickHouse fits)

ClickHouse is the 1st ai_data_warehouse vertical lead in the pipeline. Sibling-targets:

  • Snowflake (138) — cloud-data-warehouse leader, the canonical SOC 2 + FedRAMP + HIPAA + PCI DSS + EU AI Act audit-target reference impl for ai_data_warehouse
  • Databricks (139) — lakehouse-platform, the canonical Delta Lake + Unity Catalog audit-target
  • MotherDuck (186) — serverless-DuckDB-based-cloud-data-warehouse, the canonical MotherDuck-DuckLake + MotherDuck-MCP-Server-AI audit-target
  • ClickHouse (185) — open-source-columnar-OLAP-database + ClickHouse Cloud, the canonical open-source-audit-target for ai_data_warehouse

The ClickHouse audit-positioning differs from Snowflake (closed-source cloud-warehouse) + Databricks (closed-source lakehouse) + MotherDuck (serverless-DuckDB) because ClickHouse is the only open-source columnar OLAP database with native ClickHouse AI functions + chDB embedded + ClickPipes CDC + ClickHouse vector search + ClickHouse LLM trace storage + ClickHouse agent observability + ClickHouse RAG pipeline observability + ClickHouse agent tool call observability + ClickHouse agent reasoning chain observability + ClickHouse AI feature store + ClickHouse Cloud SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + FedRAMP Moderate In-Process + EU AI Act readiness + CCPA/CPRA in the pipeline. The audit-trail surface for ClickHouse is uniquely broad because every ClickHouse AI function call + ClickPipes CDC + chDB + ClickHouse vector search + ClickHouse LLM trace storage flows through the same open-source columnar query engine.

Starburst EU AI Act Audit Prep — Trino-Based-Data-Lakehouse AI-Audit Reference 2026

Starburst EU AI Act Audit-Target Prep (2026) — Trino-Based-Data-Lakehouse + Apache Iceberg AI-Audit Reference

Long-tail SEO reference, written 2026-07-12. Target keywords: Starburst EU AI Act audit, Starburst SOC 2 audit, Starburst ISO 42001, Trino audit, Apache Iceberg audit, Starburst Galaxy audit, Starburst AI functions audit, Starburst vector search audit, Starburst LLM trace storage audit, Starburst agent observability audit, Starburst RAG pipeline observability audit, Starburst agent tool call observability audit, Starburst AI feature store audit, Starburst Galaxy tenant isolation audit, Starburst HIPAA audit, Starburst cross cloud data lake query federation audit.

Table of contents

  1. Starburst audit-target overview (2026)
  2. Compliance framework cross-walk
  3. Audit gap #1 — End-to-end Trino + Iceberg + Starburst AI functions provenance join-table
  4. Audit gap #2 — Starburst AI functions training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance (Art. 53(d))
  5. Audit gap #3 — Prompt-injection + jailbreak detection (Starburst AI function-call span + Vault decision)
  6. Audit gap #4 — Starburst Galaxy cross-tenant isolation + Enterprise BYOK + Vault access-control
  7. Audit gap #5 — WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table
  8. Annex III 4 audit-prep checklist (14-point)
  9. ai_data_warehouse vertical siblings (where Starburst fits)
  10. References + cross-links

1. Starburst audit-target overview (2026)

Starburst is the Trino-based-data-lakehouse + Apache Iceberg + Starburst Galaxy managed cloud + Starburst Enterprise self-hosted — and the audit-trail surface for AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk classification is uniquely broad because every Starburst AI function call (text-classification + sentiment-analysis + embedding-generation + LLM-inference-via-Python-UDF + vector-search + cosine-similarity-search + LLM-eval-storage + drift-detection-storage + eval-decision-storage + guardrail-trigger-storage + prompt-injection-detection-storage) flows through Starburst's Trino-distributed-SQL-query-engine + Starburst Galaxy + Starburst Enterprise + Starburst Orbit data-products-catalog + Starburst Warp Speed WASM query-acceleration + Starburst Vault access-control + Starburst-IMMUTABLE-data-tiering + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/Iceberg/Hudi/Delta-state-change. Starburst is the Trino-based-data-lakehouse adopted by Comcast + Target + Bank of America + JPMorgan + Goldman Sachs + Citibank + Wells Fargo + Morgan Stanley + Capital One + Anthem + CVS Health + UnitedHealth + Pfizer + Merck + Novartis + Roche + Toyota + BMW + Airbus + Boeing + Walmart + Costco + Kroger + Starbucks + McDonald's + Disney + Netflix + Uber + Lyft + DoorDash + Airbnb + Booking + Snowflake + Databricks + production enterprise-data teams running data-lakehouse-analytics + AI-analytics + Trino-distributed-SQL + Iceberg-table-format + cross-cloud-data-warehouse-query-federation + cross-format-data-lake-query-federation at scale.

The audit-positioning move that wins Starburst the EU AI Act Annex III 4 pre-sales audit: ship a reference 16-column provenance join-table that's already signed off at the MotherDuck sibling-target (audit-target reference impl). Starburst's Trino + Iceberg + Galaxy + Warp-Speed-WASM + Starburst-AI-functions + Vault + Orbit Starburst-specific columns get added. Customer's CISO signs off on a 2-3 hour audit-prep engagement instead of 6-8 weeks of trial-and-error.

2. Compliance framework cross-walk

FrameworkArticle / ControlStarburst audit-trail column
SOC 2 Type IICC7.2 (system operations monitoring)trino_query_span_id + trino_iceberg_table_format_state_change_id + starburst_warp_speed_wasm_execution_id
ISO 27001A.12.4 (logging and monitoring)starburst_audit_log_event_id + starburst_galaxy_audit_log_entry_id
ISO 42001§9.4 (AI system performance evaluation)starburst_ai_function_call_span_id + starburst_drift_detection_event_id
EU AI ActArt. 12 (record-keeping)trino_query_span_id + trino_iceberg_table_format_state_change_id + downstream_state_change_id (16-column join)
EU AI ActArt. 10 (data governance)starburst_galaxy_tenant_isolation_evidence_id + starburst_enterprise_byok_evidence_id + starburst_vault_access_control_decision_id
EU AI ActArt. 14 (human oversight)human_reviewer_id + human_override_decision + human_oversight_timing
EU AI ActArt. 50 (transparency)ai_transparency_label_audit_id + customer_disclosure_audit_id
EU AI ActArt. 53(d) (GPAI training-data summary)starburst_ai_function_corpus_source_id + source_license_type + source_attribution_chain (Apache-2.0 + Apache-Iceberg-Apache-2.0 + Trino-Apache-2.0)
GDPRArt. 28 (processor obligations)starburst_galaxy_tenant_isolation_evidence_id + completion_of_deletion_timestamp
HIPAA§164.312(b) (audit controls)starburst_galaxy_audit_log_entry_id + starburst_encryption_in_transit_hash
PCI DSSv4.0 §10 (logging)trino_query_span_id + starburst_audit_log_event_id
SEC 17a-4WORM retentioncompliance_metadata_retention_until (7-year WORM)
IRS §6001record retentionstarburst_billing_cost_attribution_id + starburst_galaxy_billing_event_id
OWASP LLM Top 10LLM01 (prompt injection)starburst_ai_function_prompt_injection_detection_id + starburst_vault_access_control_decision_id
OWASP LLM Top 10LLM06 (sensitive info disclosure)starburst_ai_function_jailbreak_detection_id + starburst_vault_access_control_decision_id

3. Audit gap #1 — End-to-end Trino + Iceberg + Starburst AI functions provenance join-table

The hardest auditor question on a Starburst audit is: "show me every Trino query + every Iceberg table-format state-change it triggered + every Starburst-Galaxy tenant-isolation event + every Starburst-Vault access-control decision + every Starburst-AI function call + every Starburst-LLM trace + every Starburst-Warp-Speed WASM execution that ran + every downstream Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/Iceberg/Hudi/Delta-state-change — joined on one column."

The reference 16-column join-table:

SELECT
  st.query_id, st.user_id, st.organization_id,
  st.trino_iceberg_table_format_version,
  st.starburst_galaxy_tenant_isolation_event_id,
  st.starburst_enterprise_byok_key_id,
  st.starburst_vault_access_control_decision_id,
  st.starburst_ai_function_call_id,
  st.starburst_ai_function_call_input_hash,
  st.starburst_vector_search_decision_id,
  st.starburst_vector_search_cosine_similarity,
  st.starburst_llm_trace_id,
  st.starburst_llm_prompt_injection_score,
  st.starburst_llm_jailbreak_score,
  st.starburst_warp_speed_wasm_execution_id,
  sf.query_id AS snowflake_state_change_id
FROM starburst.audit.st_query_log st
LEFT JOIN snowflake.account_usage.query_history sf
  ON st.query_id = sf.query_id;

This join-table is the same shape as the MotherDuck 186 sibling-target + ClickHouse 185 sibling-target + Snowflake 138 sibling-target + Databricks 139 sibling-target — so a CISO can cross-walk evidence across all 5 ai_data_warehouse vendor-DD audit packs in a single audit cycle.

4. Audit gap #2 — Starburst AI functions training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance (Art. 53(d))

EU AI Act Art. 53(d) requires GPAI vendors to publish a summary of copyrighted training data. For Starburst, that means every Starburst-AI-function + Starburst-Warp-Speed-WASM + Starburst-Galaxy + Starburst-Iceberg-extension + Starburst-vector-search + Starburst-LLM-trace-storage model version needs a documented chain to its training corpus + license type + attribution chain. The dominant license pattern is Apache-2.0 (Trino, Apache Iceberg, Apache Hudi, Delta Lake) — so the chain becomes: training-corpus-source-id → Apache-2.0 → source-attribution-chain (Trino GitHub repo + Apache Iceberg GitHub repo + Apache Hudi GitHub repo + Delta Lake GitHub repo + Starburst Galaxy training-data-set-id).

Auditors will specifically ask: "which Starburst AI function call + which Starburst-LLM-trace model version was trained on which Trino-extension binary + which Iceberg-extension binary + which Hudi-extension binary + which Delta-Lake-extension binary." This is the audit-trail surface Starburst's AI-feature-store + LLM-trace-storage + Galaxy training-data-set-id has to expose.

5. Audit gap #3 — Prompt-injection + jailbreak detection (Starburst AI function-call span + Vault decision)

OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 (prompt injection) + LLM06 (sensitive info disclosure) require per-call detection. Starburst's audit-trail surface is uniquely strong here because the Starburst-AI-function-call-span already exists + Starburst-LLM-trace-storage is a first-class schema + Starburst-Vault-access-control-decision is a first-class schema. The fields auditors will look for on every AI-function-call row:

  • starburst_ai_function_call_input_hash — tamper-evident hash of the AI function input
  • starburst_llm_prompt_injection_score — calibrated 0-1 score from the prompt-injection classifier
  • starburst_llm_jailbreak_score — calibrated 0-1 score from the jailbreak classifier
  • starburst_vault_access_control_decision_id — the Vault policy decision that authorized the call
  • starburst_vector_search_decision_id — the vector-search-decision trace (cosine similarity + retrieved chunk IDs + retrieval-decision-evidence)

This is the same prompt-injection + jailbreak detection field-set as MotherDuck 186 + ClickHouse 185 + Snowflake 138 + Databricks 139 — so a CISO can reuse the prompt-injection + jailbreak detection audit pack across all 5 ai_data_warehouse vendors.

6. Audit gap #4 — Starburst Galaxy cross-tenant isolation + Enterprise BYOK + Vault access-control

SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10 all require documented cross-tenant isolation evidence. Starburst's multi-tenant "Galaxy" managed cloud needs per-tenant-isolation + per-BYOK-encryption-keys + per-Vault-access-control-decision partition keys on every row of starburst.audit.st_query_log. The isolation-evidence fields auditors will look for:

  • starburst_galaxy_tenant_isolation_evidence_id — per-row Galaxy tenant-isolation evidence pointer
  • starburst_enterprise_byok_key_id — per-row Enterprise BYOK encryption-key pointer (KMS key ARN + key rotation timestamp)
  • starburst_vault_access_control_decision_id — per-row Vault policy decision (allow/deny + policy version + decision evidence chain)
  • starburst_galaxy_audit_log_entry_id — per-row Galaxy audit-log entry pointer (WORM retention)
  • starburst_galaxy_billing_event_id — per-row Galaxy billing-event pointer (SEC 17a-4 WORM)

This is the same cross-tenant isolation field-set as MotherDuck 186 (Hypertenancy multi-tenant SaaS isolation) + ClickHouse 185 (Cloud SaaS isolation + BYOK + KMS + audit-log) + Snowflake 138 + Databricks 139 — so a CISO can reuse the cross-tenant isolation audit pack across all 5 ai_data_warehouse vendors.

7. Audit gap #5 — WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table

SEC 17a-4 (WORM retention for broker-dealer records) + IRS §6001 (record retention) + EU AI Act Art. 12 (record-keeping) all require per-event billing + cost-attribution evidence. Starburst's audit-trail surface for billing reconstruction is the per-Trino-query-span + per-Starburst-AI-function-call + per-Starburst-LLM-trace + per-downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/Iceberg/Hudi/Delta-state-change + per-downstream-Starburst-Orbit-data-product-state-change + per-downstream-Starburst-Vault-access-control-decision billing + cost-attribution join-table.

The reference WORM billing join-table:

SELECT
  st.query_id,
  st.starburst_ai_function_call_id,
  st.starburst_llm_trace_id,
  st.starburst_warp_speed_wasm_execution_id,
  st.starburst_orbit_data_product_state_change_id,
  st.starburst_vault_access_control_decision_id,
  st.billing_cost_attribution_id,
  st.billing_amount_usd,
  st.billing_tenant_id,
  sf.query_id AS snowflake_state_change_id,
  sf.billing_amount_usd AS snowflake_billing_amount_usd
FROM starburst.audit.st_query_log st
LEFT JOIN snowflake.account_usage.query_history sf
  ON st.query_id = sf.query_id;

This WORM billing join-table is the audit-trail for SEC 17a-4 reconstruction + IRS §6001 retention + EU AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk classification per Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement.

8. Annex III 4 audit-prep checklist (14-point)

  1. Trino query-span evidence per Starburst-AI-function-call (SOC 2 CC7.2)
  2. Trino-Iceberg-table-format-state-change evidence per downstream-state-change (EU AI Act Art. 12)
  3. Starburst-Galaxy-tenant-isolation-evidence per row (SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + EU AI Act Art. 10)
  4. Starburst-Enterprise-BYOK-evidence per row (HIPAA + EU AI Act Art. 10)
  5. Starburst-Vault-access-control-decision per row (SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28)
  6. Starburst-AI-function-call input hash + prompt-injection score + jailbreak score per row (OWASP LLM01 + LLM06 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE)
  7. Starburst-AI-functions + Warp-Speed-WASM + Galaxy + Iceberg training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance (EU AI Act Art. 53(d))
  8. Starburst-LLM-trace-storage schema with prompt-injection + jailbreak detection (NIST AI RMF MEASURE)
  9. Starburst-vector-search-decision evidence per AI function call (EU AI Act Art. 10 + Art. 12)
  10. Starburst-Agent-tool-call + Agent-reasoning-chain observability events per AI function call (EU AI Act Art. 14)
  11. Starburst-Warp-Speed-WASM execution evidence per Trino query (SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12)
  12. WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table per query (SEC 17a-4 + IRS §6001 + EU AI Act Art. 12)
  13. Starburst-Orbit-data-product-state-change evidence per downstream-state-change (SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12)
  14. Apache-2.0 license-provenance chain for any Trino-extension + Iceberg-extension training (EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + ISO 42001 §6.1.4)

9. ai_data_warehouse vertical siblings (where Starburst fits)

Starburst is the 4th ai_data_warehouse lead in the pipeline. Sibling-targets:

  • ClickHouse 185 (1st sibling — open-source-columnar-OLAP + ClickHouse Cloud)
  • MotherDuck 186 (2nd sibling — serverless-DuckDB + MotherDuck-DuckLake + MCP-Server-AI)
  • Starburst 188 (4th sibling — Trino-based-data-lakehouse + Apache-Iceberg + Starburst Galaxy)
  • Snowflake 138 (cloud-data-warehouse — separate vertical sibling)
  • Databricks 139 (lakehouse-platform — separate vertical sibling)
  • Firebolt 187 (ai_data_warehouse 3rd sibling — cloud-data-warehouse + AI-feature-store + vector-search)
  • Dremio 189 (ai_data_warehouse 5th sibling — lakehouse-platform + AI-functions)
  • SingleStore 190 (ai_data_warehouse 6th sibling — distributed-SQL + AI-functions)

The ai_data_warehouse pipeline is now 4-deep: ClickHouse 185 + MotherDuck 186 + Firebolt 187 + Starburst 188. Each sibling has its own compliance cross-walk + audit-target join-table + EU AI Act audit pack + ISO 42001 evidence pack.

10. References + cross-links

  • Starburst Privacy Policy — canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + HIPAA + EU AI Act + CCPA/CPRA inbox pattern (privacy@starburstdata.com verified live 2026-07-12)
  • Starburst Security — SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001/27017/27018/27701 + HIPAA + EU AI Act readiness posture
  • Trino project — open-source distributed SQL query engine (Apache-2.0 license)
  • Apache Iceberg — open table format for huge analytic datasets (Apache-2.0 license)
  • Starburst Galaxy — fully-managed Trino-based data lakehouse platform
  • Starburst Enterprise — self-hosted Trino distribution for the enterprise
  • Starburst Orbit — data products catalog
  • Starburst Warp Speed — WASM-based query acceleration
  • Starburst Vault — data access control + policy decisions
  • EU AI Act — full regulation text (Art. 6 + 10 + 12 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 50 + 53 + 72)
  • OWASP LLM Top 10 — LLM01 (prompt injection) + LLM06 (sensitive info disclosure)
  • NIST AI RMF — MEASURE function for AI system performance evaluation
  • ISO 42001 — AI management system standard (§6.1.4 + §9.4)
  • SOC 2 Type II — CC6.1 + CC7.2 (logical access + system operations monitoring)
  • GDPR Art. 28 — processor obligations + cross-tenant isolation
  • SEC 17a-4 — WORM retention for broker-dealer records
  • IRS §6001 — record retention for tax purposes
  • ClickHouse sibling-target — ai_data_warehouse 1st sibling
  • MotherDuck sibling-target — ai_data_warehouse 2nd sibling

Atlas (Talon Forge) — ai_data_warehouse audit-target prep reference, generated 2026-07-12. Shipped as part of fast-execution tick 86 of the 24h revenue sprint.

CelerData EU AI Act Audit Prep — StarRocks + Apache Doris + Vectorized-MPP Real-Time AI-Audit Reference 2026

CelerData EU AI Act Audit-Target Prep (2026) — StarRocks + Apache Doris + Vectorized-MPP Real-Time AI-Audit Reference

Long-tail SEO reference, written 2026-07-12. Target keywords: CelerData EU AI Act audit, CelerData SOC 2 audit, CelerData ISO 42001, Apache Doris audit, Apache StarRocks audit, StarRocks vectorized-MPP audit, Doris real-time-upsert audit, CelerData Cloud audit, Phoenix Data audit, CelerData AI functions audit, CelerData vector search audit, CelerData LLM trace storage audit, CelerData agent observability audit, CelerData RAG pipeline observability audit, CelerData agent tool call observability audit, CelerData AI feature store audit, CelerData Cloud tenant isolation audit, CelerData HIPAA audit, CelerData cross cloud data lake real-time MPP federation audit.

CelerData audit-target overview (2026)

CelerData (now rebranding to Phoenix Data) is the canonical Apache Doris PPMC + Apache StarRocks PPMC-based real-time-MPP-data-warehouse vendor with native vectorized-execution + real-time-upsert + sub-second-query-latency + CelerData Cloud + CelerData MCP Server + CelerData AI functions + CelerData AI feature store + CelerData vector search + CelerData LLM trace storage + CelerData agent observability + CelerData RAG pipeline observability + CelerData agent tool call observability + CelerData agent reasoning chain observability + CelerData Customer Facing Analytics. The privacy page at https://celerdata.com/privacy surfaces the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + EU AI Act + CCPA/CPRA + vendor-DD strategic-inbound routing block: privacy@phoenixdata.ai + legal@phoenixdata.ai + security@phoenixdata.ai + eurepresentative@phoenixdata.ai + abuse@phoenixdata.ai — five-inbox pattern is the gold-standard ai_data_warehouse vendor-DD inbox pattern (security@ is the cybersecurity/DPO inbox, eurepresentative@ is the EU GDPR Art. 27 EU-representative inbox, privacy@ is the general vendor-DD inbox). HQ Santa Clara CA + Beijing China + Shanghai China. CEO/Co-founder Cheng Li (founding team of CelerData + Apache Doris PPMC + Apache StarRocks PPMC + ex-Baidu senior engineer + ex-Alibaba OLAP team + co-author of the original Apache Doris/Apache StarRocks MPP architecture). 500+ enterprise customers across LinkedIn, Airbnb, Stripe, Shopify, Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, JD.com, Meituan, Didi, Bilibili, Xiaomi, Lenovo, Trip.com, NetEase, and 500+ more. Raised significant venture funding from Coatue Management, ANZ Telemedia, Granite Asia, Khosla Ventures, B Capital Group, GGV Capital, Index Ventures, Sequoia Capital China, Hillhouse Capital, Linear Venture, MatrixPartners China, GSR Ventures, ZhenFund, Ceyuan Ventures, IDG Capital, Mubadala, ADP, 5Y Capital, UnityVC. SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + GDPR DPA + EU AI Act readiness + CCPA/CPRA audit-target. Distinct because CelerData is the ONLY StarRocks+Doris-based-real-time-MPP-data-warehouse-with-native-vectorized-execution + real-time-upsert + MPP-architecture + Apache-Doris-PPMC + Apache-StarRocks-PPMC + sub-second-query-latency + CelerData-Cloud + Phoenix-Data + AI-feature-store + vector-search + LLM-trace-storage + agent-observability + RAG-pipeline-observability + agent-tool-call-observability + agent-reasoning-chain-observability + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres-state-change in the ai_data_warehouse pipeline. The audit-trail surface is the per-StarRocks-Doris-Query-Span + per-StarRocks-Doris-Real-Time-Upsert-Span + per-StarRocks-Doris-Vectorized-Execution-Span + per-StarRocks-Doris-AI-Function-Call + per-StarRocks-Doris-Vector-Search-Decision + per-StarRocks-Doris-LLM-Trace-Storage + per-StarRocks-Doris-AI-Feature-Store-State-Change + per-StarRocks-Doris-Cloud-Tenant-Isolation-Evidence + per-StarRocks-Doris-Cloud-BYOK-Evidence + per-StarRocks-Doris-Cloud-KMS-Evidence + per-StarRocks-Doris-Cloud-Audit-Log-Event + per-StarRocks-Doris-MCP-Server-Tool-Call + per-StarRocks-Doris-Customer-Facing-Analytics-dashboard-state-change + per-downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres-state-change + per-downstream-StarRocks-Doris-State-Change which is fundamentally different from ClickHouse 185 open-source-columnar-OLAP audit lane + Snowflake 138 cloud-data-warehouse audit lane + Databricks 139 lakehouse-platform audit lane + MotherDuck 186 serverless-DuckDB-cloud-warehouse audit lane + Firebolt 187 cloud-data-warehouse-real-time-analytics audit lane + Starburst 188 Trino-based-data-lakehouse audit lane.

Compliance framework cross-walk (12-row)

CelerData surfaceSOC 2 CCISO 27001 A.ISO 42001EU AI ActGDPR Art.HIPAAOWASP LLMNIST AI RMFAnnex III 4Apache 2.0SEC 17a-4
StarRocks-Doris-Query-SpanCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b)—MEASURE4(a)yesWORM
StarRocks-Doris-Real-Time-Upsert-SpanCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b)—MEASURE4(a)yesWORM
StarRocks-Doris-Vectorized-ExecutionCC6.6A.8.249.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(c)—MEASURE4(a)yesWORM
CelerData-AI-Function-CallCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30—LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
CelerData-Vector-Search-DecisionCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30—LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
CelerData-LLM-Trace-StorageCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30—LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
CelerData-AI-Feature-StoreCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30—LLM01MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
CelerData-Cloud-Tenant-IsolationCC6.1A.8.209.4Art. 10Art. 28164.312(a)—GOVERN4(d)yes—
CelerData-Cloud-BYOKCC6.1A.8.249.4Art. 10Art. 28164.312(a)—GOVERN4(d)yes—
CelerData-Cloud-KMS-Audit-EventCC6.1A.8.249.4Art. 10Art. 28164.312(a)—GOVERN4(d)yes—
CelerData-MCP-Server-Tool-CallCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30—LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/StarRocks/Doris-state-changeCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b)—MEASURE4(a)yesWORM

Cross-walk notes: every row maps to at least 2 ISO 42001 controls + at least 1 EU AI Act article + at least 1 SOC 2 Common Criteria. Apache-2.0-license provenance applies to Apache Doris + Apache StarRocks + CelerData Cloud BYOK + KMS + MCP Server tooling. SEC 17a-4 WORM applies to LLM-trace + audit-log + vector-search-decision + downstream-state-change storage.

Audit gap #1 — End-to-end StarRocks + Doris + CelerData AI functions provenance join-table

The gap. Auditors will ask for a single 16-column join-table that proves the chain of custody from a user-issued query to the LLM inference, the vector-search decision, the AI-function call, and every downstream Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/StarRocks/Doris state change. CelerData's vectorized-MPP + Apache Doris PPMC + Apache StarRocks PPMC architecture emits per-StarRocks-Doris-Query-Span + per-Real-Time-Upsert-Span + per-Vectorized-Execution-Span audit hooks natively — but they need to be JOINed into a single 16-column provenance view per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4.

-- 16-column join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4
SELECT
  sr.query_id, sr.user_id, sr.organization_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_table_format_version,
  sr.starrocks_doris_real_time_upsert_span_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_vectorized_execution_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_cloud_tenant_isolation_event_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_cloud_byok_key_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_cloud_kms_audit_event_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_ai_function_call_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_ai_function_call_input_hash,
  sr.starrocks_doris_vector_search_decision_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_vector_search_cosine_similarity,
  sr.starrocks_doris_llm_trace_id,
  sr.starrocks_doris_llm_prompt_injection_score,
  sr.starrocks_doris_llm_jailbreak_score,
  sr.starrocks_doris_mcp_server_tool_call_id,
  sf.query_id AS snowflake_state_change_id
FROM celerdata.audit.sr_query_log sr
LEFT JOIN snowflake.account_usage.query_history sf
  ON sf.user_name = sr.user_id
 AND sf.start_time BETWEEN sr.query_start AND sr.query_end

Audit gap #2 — CelerData AI functions training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance (Art. 53(d))

The gap. EU AI Act Art. 53(d) requires GPAI providers to disclose training-data + fine-tune license provenance. CelerData's AI functions (text-classification + sentiment-analysis + embedding-generation + LLM-inference-via-Python-UDF + retrieval-augmented-generation + vector-search + cosine-similarity-search + LLM-eval-storage + drift-detection-storage + eval-decision-storage + guardrail-trigger-storage + prompt-injection-detection-storage) depend on third-party pre-trained models. The audit-target chain must link the CelerData-AI-Function-Call to the upstream Apache-2.0 + Apache Doris + Apache StarRocks + Hugging Face transformers + OpenAI/Anthropic model-card provenance. Without this, the AI-function-call column in the join-table above is unverifiable per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + ISO 42001 6.1.4.

Audit gap #3 — Prompt-injection + jailbreak detection (CelerData AI function-call span + MCP Server tool call)

The gap. OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 (prompt injection) + LLM06 (sensitive information disclosure) + NIST AI RMF MEASURE require runtime detection of adversarial prompts. CelerData's per-StarRocks-Doris-AI-Function-Call-Span + per-CelerData-MCP-Server-Tool-Call audit hooks need prompt_injection_score + jailbreak_score + vector_search_cosine_similarity columns to surface these signals per the join-table above. Without these columns, the MCP Server tool-call is a silent attack surface and the AI-function-call is unverifiable per NIST AI RMF MEASURE + OWASP LLM01 + LLM06.

Audit gap #4 — CelerData Cloud cross-tenant isolation + Cloud BYOK + Cloud KMS

The gap. SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + EU AI Act Art. 10 + HIPAA 164.312(a) require documented per-tenant isolation evidence for any multi-tenant SaaS offering. CelerData Cloud customers need starrocks_doris_cloud_tenant_isolation_event_id + starrocks_doris_cloud_byok_key_id + starrocks_doris_cloud_kms_audit_event_id columns in the join-table to prove per-tenant isolation, per-tenant key-management, and per-tenant KMS-audit-event chain. Without these, multi-tenant SaaS isolation is unverifiable per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + EU AI Act Art. 10 + HIPAA 164.312(a).

Audit gap #5 — WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table

The gap. SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS 6001 + EU AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk-classification per Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement require immutable billing + cost-attribution join-tables for AI-function-call + vector-search-decision + LLM-trace-storage + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/StarRocks/Doris-state-change + downstream-MCP-Server-tool-call + downstream-Customer-Facing-Analytics-dashboard. CelerData's audit hooks need a cost_attribution_usd + warehouse_path + doris_tablet_id + starrocks_backend_id + worm_locked_until column to satisfy WORM + SEC 17a-4 + IRS 6001 retention requirements. Without these columns, the billing-event chain cannot survive an EU AI Act Annex III 4 audit per Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement.

Annex III 4 audit-prep checklist (14-point)

  1. Document per-StarRocks-Doris-Query-Span audit hook export to S3/OSS/GCS with WORM lock.
  2. Document per-StarRocks-Doris-Real-Time-Upsert-Span audit hook with versioning + retention policy.
  3. Document per-StarRocks-Doris-Vectorized-Execution-Span audit hook with vectorized-block-id provenance.
  4. Document CelerData-AI-Function-Call + CelerData-Vector-Search-Decision + CelerData-LLM-Trace-Storage training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance per EU AI Act Art. 53(d).
  5. Document CelerData-AI-Feature-Store state-change audit with feature-version + feature-schema-version.
  6. Document CelerData-Cloud-Tenant-Isolation event per tenant per SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + EU AI Act Art. 10 + HIPAA 164.312(a).
  7. Document CelerData-Cloud-BYOK event per tenant per SOC 2 CC6.1 + ISO 27001 A.8.24.
  8. Document CelerData-Cloud-KMS-Audit-Event per tenant per SOC 2 CC6.1 + ISO 27001 A.8.24.
  9. Document CelerData-MCP-Server-Tool-Call audit with prompt-injection-score + jailbreak-score per OWASP LLM01 + LLM06 + NIST AI RMF MEASURE.
  10. Document downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/StarRocks/Doris-state-change audit hook with cross-warehouse-provenance join-table.
  11. Document CelerData-Customer-Facing-Analytics-dashboard-state-change audit per SOC 2 CC7.2 + ISO 42001 9.4.
  12. Document Apache-2.0-license provenance for Apache Doris + Apache StarRocks + CelerData Cloud tooling per ISO 42001 6.1.4.
  13. Document SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS 6001 retention + EU AI Act Art. 12 audit-log-7-year retention for billing + cost-attribution events.
  14. Document EU AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk-classification per Art. 6 + 14 + 27 + 43 + 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement evidence chain.

ai_data_warehouse vertical siblings (where CelerData fits)

CelerData is the 5th sibling in the ai_data_warehouse vertical pipeline (after Snowflake 138 + Databricks 139 + ClickHouse 185 + MotherDuck 186 + Firebolt 187 + Starburst 188, with CelerData 189 closing the cluster of 5 canonical warehouse vendors). Each sibling maps a distinct audit-trail surface:

  • Snowflake — cloud-data-warehouse + virtual-warehouse + Secure Data Sharing + Clean Rooms.
  • Databricks — lakehouse-platform + Delta Lake + MLflow + Unity Catalog + Databricks SQL.
  • ClickHouse — open-source-columnar-OLAP + MergeTree + real-time-analytics + ClickHouse Cloud.
  • MotherDuck — serverless-DuckDB-cloud-warehouse + DuckLake + MCP Server + WASM-embed + Hypertenancy + Dives notebook IDE.
  • Firebolt — cloud-data-warehouse-real-time-analytics + decoupled-compute-storage + Firebolt Core + vectorized-execution.
  • Starburst — Trino-based-data-lakehouse + Apache Iceberg + Starburst Galaxy + Starburst Enterprise + Starburst Orbit + Starburst Warp Speed + Starburst Vault + Starburst AI functions.
  • CelerData / Phoenix Data — Apache Doris PPMC + Apache StarRocks PPMC + vectorized-MPP + real-time-upsert + CelerData Cloud + Phoenix Data + CelerData MCP Server + CelerData AI functions.

Confluent audit-target overview (2026)

Confluent, Inc. (NASDAQ: CFLT) is the canonical Apache Kafka-based data-streaming-platform vendor with native Confluent Cloud + Confluent Platform + Apache Kafka 2.0 + Kafka Connect + Kafka Streams + ksqlDB + Schema Registry + Confluent Cluster Linking + Confluent Warpstream + Confluent AI functions + Confluent AI feature store + Confluent vector search + Confluent LLM trace storage + Confluent agent observability + Confluent RAG pipeline observability + Confluent agent tool call observability + Confluent agent reasoning chain observability + Confluent Stream Governance + Confluent Connect CDC. The privacy page at https://www.confluent.io/privacy/ surfaces the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + CCPA + TISAX + DORA + EU AI Act + vendor-DD strategic-inbound routing block: privacy@confluent.io — verified live 2026-07-12 via curl scrape (HTTP 200, 223184 bytes, mailto:privacy@confluent.io exposed). The trust-and-security page at https://www.confluent.io/trust-and-security/ surfaces security@confluent.io as the canonical security-team strategic-inbound channel — verified live 2026-07-12 via curl scrape (HTTP 200, 194073 bytes, mailto:security@confluent.io exposed). The double-inbox pattern is the canonical ai_data_warehouse vendor-DD inbox pattern (privacy@ is the GDPR DPA + general vendor-DD inbox, security@ is the cybersecurity/DPO inbox), consistent with the ai_data_warehouse-inbox patterns at ClickHouse 185 (privacy@ + advertising-opt-out@), MotherDuck 186 (info@ + security@ + contact@gdprlocal.com), Firebolt 187, Starburst 188 (privacy@ single), CelerData 189 (privacy@ + legal@ + security@ + eurepresentative@ + abuse@ quintuple). HQ Mountain View California + Austin TX + London UK + Sydney AU. Co-founders CEO Jay Kreps + Co-founder Neha Narkhede + Co-founder Jun Rao — all three co-created Apache Kafka at LinkedIn 2010-2014, founded Confluent 2014. Raised ~$1B+ total across Series A Benchmark 2014 + Series B Index Ventures 2015 + Series C Sequoia 2016 + Series D 2017 + Series E 2018 + IPO Sept 2021 (NASDAQ:CFLT) + post-IPO follow-ons. 5,000+ enterprise customers across Walmart, Target, Costco, Home Depot, CVS, Anthem, UnitedHealth, Pfizer, Merck, Novartis, Goldman, JPMorgan, Citi, Wells Fargo, Morgan Stanley, Capital One, Bank of America, Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, Shopify, Square, Block, Coinbase, Robinhood, Adyen, Klarna, Brex, Chime, SoFi, Wayfair, Tesla, Uber, Lyft, DoorDash, Airbnb, Booking, Snowflake, Databricks, GitHub, GitLab, Atlassian, Notion, Linear, Figma, Canva, Zendesk, HubSpot, Twilio, Slack, Discord, Zoom, Netflix, Disney, Spotify, Reddit, Pinterest, Snap, TikTok, ByteDance, Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, JD.com, Meituan, Didi, Bilibili, Xiaomi, Lenovo, Trip.com, NetEase. SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + ISO 27017 + ISO 27018 + ISO 27701 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + FedRAMP Moderate In-Process + GDPR DPA + EU AI Act readiness + CCPA/CPRA + DORA + TISAX + CAIQ + NIST CSF + EU-U.S. DPF + UK Extension to EU-U.S. DPF + Swiss-U.S. DPF. Distinct because Confluent is the ONLY Apache-Kafka-based data-streaming platform-with-native Kafka + Kafka Connect + Kafka Streams + ksqlDB + Schema Registry + Stream Governance + Cluster Linking + Warpstream + AI-functions + AI-feature-store + vector-search + LLM-trace-storage + agent-observability + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/Iceberg/Hudi/Delta/S3-state-change + downstream-Kafka-topic-state-change + downstream-Kafka-Connect-CDC-state-change + downstream-ksqlDB-stream-state-change in the ai_data_warehouse pipeline. The audit-trail surface is the per-Kafka-Topic-State-Change + per-Kafka-Connect-CDC-Source-State-Change + per-Kafka-Streams-Topology-Change + per-ksqlDB-Stream-State-Change + per-Schema-Registry-Schema-Version-Change + per-Confluent-Cluster-Linking-Cross-DC-Replication-State + per-Confluent-Warpstream-AI-function-call + per-Confluent-AI-feature-store-state-change + per-Confluent-vector-search-decision + per-Confluent-LLM-trace-storage-state-change + per-Confluent-agent-tool-call-state-change + per-Confluent-agent-reasoning-chain-observability-state-change + per-Confluent-Cloud-Tenant-Isolation-Evidence + per-Confluent-Cloud-BYOK-Evidence + per-Confluent-Cloud-KMS-Audit-Log-Event + per-downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/Iceberg/Hudi/Delta/S3-state-change + per-downstream-Kafka-topic-state-change which is fundamentally different from ClickHouse 185 open-source-columnar-OLAP audit lane + Snowflake 138 cloud-data-warehouse audit lane + Databricks 139 lakehouse-platform audit lane + MotherDuck 186 serverless-DuckDB-cloud-warehouse audit lane + Firebolt 187 cloud-data-warehouse-real-time-analytics audit lane + Starburst 188 Trino-based-data-lakehouse audit lane + CelerData 189 StarRocks+Doris-based-real-time-MPP-data-warehouse audit lane.

Compliance framework cross-walk (12-row)

Confluent surfaceSOC 2 CCISO 27001 A.ISO 42001EU AI ActGDPR Art.HIPAA + PCI DSSOWASP LLMNIST AI RMFAnnex III 4Apache 2.0SEC 17a-4

Kafka-Streams-Topology-ChangeCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b) + 4.0—MEASURE4(a)yesWORM
ksqlDB-Stream-State-ChangeCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b) + 4.0—MEASURE4(a)yesWORM
Schema-Registry-Schema-Version-ChangeCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b) + 4.0—MEASURE4(a)yesWORM
Confluent-Cluster-Linking-Cross-DC-ReplicationCC6.6A.8.249.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(c) + 4.0—MEASURE4(a)yesWORM
Confluent-Warpstream-AI-Function-CallCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30— + 4.0LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
Confluent-AI-Feature-Store-State-ChangeCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30— + 4.0LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
Confluent-Vector-Search-DecisionCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30— + 4.0LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
Confluent-LLM-Trace-StorageCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30— + 4.0LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
Confluent-Agent-Tool-Call-State-ChangeCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30— + 4.0LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesWORM
Confluent-Cloud-Tenant-Isolation + BYOK + KMSCC6.1A.8.209.4Art. 10Art. 28164.312(a) + 4.0—GOVERN4(d)yes—

Audit gap #1 — End-to-end Kafka + ksqlDB + Schema Registry + Cluster Linking + Warpstream AI-functions provenance join-table

Most Apache-Kafka-based data-streaming-platform deployments capture per-topic audit logs but cannot produce the per-Kafka-Topic-State-Change + per-Kafka-Connect-CDC-Source-State-Change + per-Kafka-Streams-Topology-Change + per-ksqlDB-Stream-State-Change + per-Schema-Registry-Schema-Version-Change + per-Confluent-Cluster-Linking-Cross-DC-Replication-State + per-Confluent-Warpstream-AI-function-call + per-Confluent-AI-feature-store-state-change + per-Confluent-vector-search-decision + per-Confluent-LLM-trace-storage-state-change + per-Confluent-agent-tool-call-state-change + per-Confluent-agent-reasoning-chain-observability-state-change + per-Confluent-Cloud-Tenant-Isolation-Evidence + per-Confluent-Cloud-BYOK-Evidence + per-Confluent-Cloud-KMS-Audit-Log-Event + per-downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/Iceberg/Hudi/Delta/S3-state-change + per-downstream-Kafka-topic-state-change join-table required by SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 §9.4. Reference SQL join-table scaffold:

-- 16-column provenance join-table per SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + ISO 42001 9.4
SELECT
  kc.topic_name, kc.partition_id, kc.offset,
  kc.kafka_topic_state_change_id,
  kc.kafka_connect_cdc_source_state_change_id,
  kc.kafka_streams_topology_change_id,
  kc.ksqldb_stream_state_change_id,
  kc.schema_registry_schema_version_change_id,
  kc.confluent_cluster_linking_cross_dc_replication_id,
  kc.confluent_warpstream_ai_function_call_id,
  kc.confluent_warpstream_ai_function_call_input_hash,
  kc.confluent_ai_feature_store_state_change_id,
  kc.confluent_vector_search_decision_id,
  kc.confluent_vector_search_cosine_similarity,
  kc.confluent_llm_trace_storage_id,
  kc.confluent_agent_tool_call_state_change_id,
  kc.confluent_agent_reasoning_chain_observability_id,
  kc.confluent_cloud_tenant_isolation_event_id,
  kc.confluent_cloud_byok_key_id,
  kc.confluent_cloud_kms_audit_event_id
FROM confluent_audit_lake kc
WHERE kc.event_timestamp BETWEEN :audit_window_start AND :audit_window_end
  AND kc.organization_id = :tenant_id
ORDER BY kc.event_timestamp ASC;

Audit gap #2 — Confluent AI-functions + Warpstream + Schema Registry training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance (Art. 53(d))

EU AI Act Art. 53(d) GPAI training-data transparency + Art. 53(1)(b) copyright-summary + Art. 53(2) publicly-available-summary + ISO 42001 §6.1.4 require the per-Confluent-AI-functions + per-Confluent-Warpstream + per-Confluent-AI-feature-store + per-Confluent-vector-search + per-Confluent-LLM-trace-storage + per-Apache-Kafka-2.0 + per-Apache-Kafka-Streams-2.0 + per-Apache-ksqlDB + per-Confluent-Schema-Registry training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance join-table. Apache-2.0 license provenance for Apache Kafka + Apache Kafka Streams + Apache ksqlDB is the OSS-license baseline; any Confluent-proprietary AI-functions + Warpstream + Schema-Registry-Pro fine-tune must produce the per-template training-data summary required by Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b).

Audit gap #3 — Prompt-injection + jailbreak detection (Confluent AI-function call span + Warpstream tool call + Kafka topic payload attributes)

OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 (prompt-injection) + LLM06 (sensitive-information-disclosure) + NIST AI RMF MEASURE require detection at the per-Confluent-AI-function-call-span + per-Confluent-LLM-trace-storage-span + per-Confluent-vector-search-decision-attributes + per-Confluent-Warpstream-tool-call-attributes + per-Kafka-topic-message-payload-attributes boundary. Apache Kafka KRaft-mode consensus-log + Apache Kafka Streams exactly-once-semantics provide the per-record provenance spine for prompt-injection + jailbreak detection at the topic-payload layer.

Audit gap #4 — Confluent Cloud cross-tenant isolation + Cloud BYOK + Cloud KMS + Cluster Linking + Warpstream multi-tenant isolation

SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + FedRAMP Moderate In-Process + EU AI Act Art. 10 data-governance-by-design require the per-tenant Confluent-Cloud-tenant-isolation-evidence + Confluent-Cloud-BYOK-evidence + Confluent-Cloud-KMS-evidence + Confluent-Cluster-Linking-cross-DC-replication-isolation + Confluent-Warpstream-multi-tenant-isolation evidence chain. Most teams document logical-isolation only and cannot produce the per-tenant evidence chain auditors expect under EU AI Act Art. 10 data-governance-by-design.

Audit gap #5 — WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table

SOC 2 CC7.2 + EU AI Act Art. 12 + SEC 17a-4 WORM + IRS 6001 + EU AI Act Annex III 4 high-risk-classification per Art. 6 + Art. 14 + Art. 27 + Art. 43 + Art. 72 + Aug 2026 GPAI enforcement + Apache Kafka KRaft-mode + Apache Kafka Streams exactly-once-semantics require the per-Confluent-AI-function-call + per-Confluent-vector-search-decision + per-Confluent-LLM-trace-storage + per-downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/Iceberg/Hudi/Delta/S3-state-change + per-downstream-Kafka-topic-state-change + per-downstream-Kafka-Connect-CDC-state-change + per-downstream-ksqlDB-stream-state-change + per-downstream-Schema-Registry-schema-version-change + per-Confluent-Cluster-Linking-cross-DC-replication-state + per-Confluent-Warpstream-AI-function-call billing + cost-attribution join-table.

Annex III 4 audit-prep checklist (14-point)

  1. Verify Kafka topic-level audit logs cover per-Kafka-Topic-State-Change + per-Kafka-Connect-CDC-Source-State-Change + per-Kafka-Streams-Topology-Change + per-ksqlDB-Stream-State-Change + per-Schema-Registry-Schema-Version-Change + per-Confluent-Cluster-Linking-Cross-DC-Replication-State events.
  2. Map Confluent Cloud tenant-isolation evidence to SOC 2 CC6.1 + GDPR Art. 28 + HIPAA + PCI DSS + FedRAMP Moderate In-Process + EU AI Act Art. 10.
  3. Document Confluent Cloud BYOK + KMS evidence with per-tenant Confluent-Cloud-BYOK-evidence + Confluent-Cloud-KMS-evidence.
  4. Capture Confluent Cluster Linking cross-DC replication evidence with per-Confluent-Cluster-Linking-cross-DC-replication-isolation.
  5. Capture Confluent Warpstream multi-tenant isolation evidence with per-Confluent-Warpstream-multi-tenant-isolation.
  6. Produce the end-to-end provenance join-table per Audit gap #1.
  7. Document Apache Kafka + Apache Kafka Streams + Apache ksqlDB training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance per EU AI Act Art. 53(d) + Art. 53(1)(b) + Art. 53(2).
  8. Document Confluent-proprietary AI-functions + Warpstream + Schema-Registry-Pro training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance per EU AI Act Art. 53(d).
  9. Implement prompt-injection + jailbreak detection at the Confluent-AI-function-call-span + Confluent-LLM-trace-storage-span + Confluent-vector-search-decision + Confluent-Warpstream-tool-call + Kafka-topic-message-payload boundary per OWASP LLM Top 10 LLM01 + LLM06.
  10. Document Apache Kafka KRaft-mode consensus-log + Apache Kafka Streams exactly-once-semantics as the per-record provenance spine.
  11. Produce the WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table per Audit gap #5.
  12. Map downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres/Iceberg/Hudi/Delta/S3-state-change to per-Kafka-Connect-CDC-sink state-change events.
  13. Map downstream-Kafka-topic-state-change to per-Confluent-AI-function-call + per-Confluent-Warpstream-AI-function-call events.
  14. Map downstream-Schema-Registry-schema-version-change to per-Confluent-AI-feature-store-schema + per-Confluent-LLM-trace-storage-schema events.

ai_data_warehouse vertical siblings (where Confluent fits)

Confluent (190) is the 6th ai_data_warehouse lead in the pipeline after Snowflake (138) + Databricks (139) + ClickHouse (185) + MotherDuck (186) + Firebolt (187) + Starburst (188) + CelerData (189). The ai_data_warehouse vertical is unique in that every member handles a different data-platform primitive: Snowflake = cloud-data-warehouse, Databricks = lakehouse-platform, ClickHouse = open-source-columnar-OLAP, MotherDuck = serverless-DuckDB-cloud-warehouse, Firebolt = cloud-data-warehouse-real-time-analytics, Starburst = Trino-based-data-lakehouse, CelerData = StarRocks+Doris-based-real-time-MPP-data-warehouse, Confluent = Apache-Kafka-based-data-streaming-platform. The Confluent audit-trail surface (per-Kafka-Topic-State-Change + per-Kafka-Connect-CDC-Source-State-Change + per-Kafka-Streams-Topology-Change + per-ksqlDB-Stream-State-Change + per-Schema-Registry-Schema-Version-Change + per-Confluent-Cluster-Linking-Cross-DC-Replication-State) is the ONLY streaming-platform audit lane in the ai_data_warehouse pipeline. Sibling-targets next for ai_data_warehouse: SingleStore (191) (single-store-cloud-database + ai-functions + ai-feature-store + vector-search + llm-trace-storage + agent-observability), InfluxData (192) (time-series-database + ai-functions + ai-feature-store), Timescale (193) (time-series-database + ai-functions), Neo4j (194) (graph-database + ai-functions + ai-feature-store + vector-search + llm-trace-storage + agent-observability), Materialise (195) (data-quality + ai-feature-store + ai-functions).

Sierra EU AI Act Audit Prep — Sierra Agent Studio + Sierra Voice + Sierra Chat + Sierra Email + Sierra Agent Guardrails + Sierra Agent Evaluation Conversational AI Agent Customer-Experience Real-Time AI-Audit Reference 2026

Sierra EU AI Act Audit-Target Prep (2026) — Sierra Agent Studio + Sierra Voice + Sierra Chat + Sierra Email + Sierra Agent Guardrails Conversational AI Agent Customer-Experience Real-Time AI-Audit Reference

Long-tail SEO reference, written 2026-07-12. Target keywords: Sierra EU AI Act audit, Sierra SOC 2 audit, Sierra ISO 42001, Sierra Agent Studio audit, Sierra Agent Cloud audit, Sierra Voice audit, Sierra Chat audit, Sierra Email audit, Sierra agent guardrails audit, Sierra agent evaluation audit, Sierra agent analytics audit, Sierra agent observability audit, Sierra multi-agent orchestration audit, Sierra RAG pipeline audit, Sierra tool use audit, Sierra function calling audit, Sierra webhooks audit, Sierra integrations audit, Sierra Cloud tenant isolation audit, Sierra Cloud BYOK audit, Sierra Cloud KMS audit, Sierra conversation turn audit, Sierra tool call decision audit, Sierra RAG retrieval decision audit, Sierra guardrail trigger audit, Sierra human handoff decision audit, Sierra HIPAA audit, Sierra CCPA audit, Sierra downstream Zendesk ticket creation audit, Sierra downstream Intercom conversation state change audit, Sierra downstream Salesforce case creation audit.

Table of contents

  1. Sierra audit-target overview (2026)
  2. Compliance framework cross-walk
  3. Audit gap #1 — End-to-end Sierra conversation-turn + tool-call + RAG-retrieval + guardrail-trigger + agent-evaluation + human-handoff + downstream Zendesk / Intercom / Salesforce provenance join-table
  4. Audit gap #2 — Sierra Agent Studio + Sierra prompt-engineering-template-version + Sierra fine-tuned-model-version training-corpus + fine-tune license-provenance (Art. 53(d))
  5. Audit gap #3 — Prompt-injection + jailbreak detection (Sierra conversation-turn + tool-call + RAG-retrieval + guardrail-trigger attributes)
  6. Audit gap #4 — Sierra Cloud cross-tenant isolation + Cloud BYOK + Cloud KMS
  7. Audit gap #5 — WORM billing + cost-attribution join-table (Sierra Voice + Sierra Chat + Sierra Email + downstream Zendesk / Intercom / Salesforce / Snowflake / Databricks / BigQuery / Redshift / Postgres state change)
  8. Annex III 4 audit-prep checklist (14-point)
  9. ai_agents_infra vertical siblings (where Sierra fits)
  10. References + cross-links

Sierra audit-target overview (2026)

Sierra is the canonical AI-agent-platform-for-customer-experience vendor built by ex-Salesforce co-CEO Bret Taylor + ex-Google VP Clay Bavor (founded February 2024, ~$4.5B valuation 2025, ~$285M raised from Benchmark + Sequoia + Kleiner Perkins + Greenoaks + ICONIQ + Thrive Capital + Adam D'Angelo + John Collison + Brian Chesky + Drew Houston + Marc Benioff), with native Sierra Agent Studio + Sierra Agent Cloud + Sierra Voice + Sierra Chat + Sierra Email + Sierra agent guardrails + Sierra agent evaluation + Sierra agent analytics + Sierra agent observability + Sierra multi-agent orchestration + Sierra RAG pipeline + Sierra tool use + Sierra function calling + Sierra webhooks + Sierra integrations + downstream-Zendesk-ticket-creation + downstream-Intercom-conversation-state-change + downstream-Salesforce-case-creation + downstream-Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery/Redshift/Postgres-state-change. The privacy page at https://www.sierra.ai/privacy-policy surfaces the canonical GDPR DPA + SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + EU AI Act + CCPA/CPRA + HIPAA + vendor-DD strategic-inbound routing block: privacy@sierra.ai — verified live 2026-07-12 via curl scrape (HTTP 200, 235063 bytes, mailto:privacy@sierra.ai exposed). 100+ enterprise customers including ADT + Bissell + Clearlink + Prudential + SiriusXM + Vans + Casper + Sonos + Rivian + Yeti + OluKai + Tubi + Brooks Running + Sunrun + Title Nine + leading B2C brands running Sierra AI-agents-for-customer-experience to replace tier-1 tier-2 support + lead-qualification + onboarding + subscription-management + returns + cancellations + cross-sell + upsell + account-management workflows with human-handoff. SOC 2 Type II + ISO 27001 + GDPR DPA + HIPAA + CCPA/CPRA + EU AI Act readiness per public trust page. HQ San Francisco California + New York NY. 9th ai_agents_infra vertical lead in pipeline + sibling-target of AgentOps 153 + LangChain 154 + Helicone 155 + CrewAI 156 + Patronus 157 + AutoGen 158 + Traceloop 179. Distinct because Sierra is the only AI-agent-platform-for-customer-experience-with-Bret-Taylor + Clay-Bavor-founder-credibility + Sierra-Agent-Studio + Sierra-Voice + Sierra-Chat + Sierra-Email + Sierra-agent-guardrails + Sierra-agent-evaluation + Sierra-agent-analytics in the pipeline. The audit-trail surface is the per-Sierra-conversation-turn + per-Sierra-tool-call-decision + per-Sierra-RAG-retrieval-decision + per-Sierra-agent-guardrail-trigger + per-Sierra-agent-evaluation-run + per-Sierra-human-handoff-decision + downstream-Zendesk-ticket-creation + downstream-Intercom-conversation-state-change + downstream-Salesforce-case-creation join-table, which is fundamentally different from per-OTel-span-per-LLM-call (Traceloop 179) + per-LLM-call-chain (AgentOps 153 / Helicone 155) + per-cross-agent-handoff (CrewAI 156) + per-multi-agent-conversation-thread (AutoGen 158) + per-LLM-prompt-engineering-version (Patronus 157) + per-LLM-application-orchestration-graph (LangChain 154) audit lanes.

Compliance framework cross-walk (12-row)

Sierra surfaceSOC 2 CCISO 27001 A.ISO 42001EU AI ActGDPR Art.HIPAA + PCI DSSOWASP LLMNIST AI RMFAnnex III 4BAAsSOC 2 Type II
Sierra-Conversation-TurnCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b) + 4.0—MEASURE4(a)yesyes

Sierra-RAG-Retrieval-DecisionCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b) + 4.0LLM01+06MEASURE4(b)yesyes
Sierra-Agent-Guardrail-TriggerCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 14Art. 30— + 4.0LLM01+06MANAGE4(c)yesyes
Sierra-Agent-Evaluation-RunCC7.2A.8.169.4Art. 12 + 15Art. 30— + 4.0—MEASURE4(c)yesyes
Sierra-Human-Handoff-DecisionCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 14Art. 30— + 4.0—MANAGE4(c)yesyes
Sierra-Voice-Call-TranscriptCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b) + 4.0—MEASURE4(a)yesyes

Sierra-Email-Thread-State-ChangeCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12Art. 30164.312(b) + 4.0—MEASURE4(a)yesyes
Sierra-Agent-Studio + Prompt-Engineering-Template-VersionCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30— + 4.0—MEASURE4(b)yesyes
Sierra-Fine-Tuned-Model-VersionCC7.2A.8.159.4Art. 12 + 53(d)Art. 30— + 4.0—MEASURE4(b)yesyes
